articles.title: Gold price today: Gold rate falls 1%, silver crashes 2% on MCX on a stronger dollar, Fed rate-hike bets
articles.summary: As per MCX, domestic spot gold prices dropped by ₹15,100, or nearly 10%, in June to come to ₹1,40,864 per 10 grams, while silver rates declined by ₹38,250, or almost 15%, in June to settle at ₹2,25,125 per kg on 30 June.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: MFs wary of Sebi plan to allow celeb ads, cite mis-selling, edge for big entities
articles.summary: Sebi's proposal to let mutual fund houses use celebrity endorsements has sparked a debate over investor protection and competition. While supporters say it could bring in more first-time investors, critics fear higher mis-selling risks and a growing advantage for large fund houses.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Here are the stocks where the government could do OFS

articles.title: Aastha Spintex IPO Day 3: Last day to buy; check GMP, subscription status, and other details - Apply or not?
articles.summary: The Aastha Spintex IPO opened for subscription on June 29, seeking ₹170 crore by issuing 1.25 crore shares at ₹125-136 each. The IPO allotment is on July 2, and shares are anticipated to list on July 6 on BSE and NSE.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stock market today: Gift Nifty hints flat start; US-Iran talks, weak yen to crude oil prices - 8 stocks to buy or sell
articles.summary: The Indian stock market may open quietly on Wednesday, influenced by global market trends. Domestic indices dropped yesterday, with the Nifty 50 and BSE Sensex both closing lower. Oil prices rose slightly amid ongoing geopolitical tensions between the US and Iran.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Buy or sell: Gift Nifty signals positive start, Vaishali Parekh recommends three stocks to buy today — 1 July 2026
articles.summary: Buy or sell: Vaishali Parekh recommends three stocks to buy today — Suzlon Energy, Marksans Pharma, and Cochin Shipyard

articles.title: Crude oil prices gain amid US-Iran talks uncertainty; Brent crude at $73 per barrel
articles.summary: US-Iran war: Brent crude futures gained 50 cents, or 0.69%, to $73.45 a barrel, while US West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude rose 63 cents, or 0.91%, to $70.13 a barrel.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Asian stocks today: Nikkei 225, Kospi rise more than 1% on US tech rally, optimism over US-Iran talks
articles.summary: Asian stocks today: Japan's Nikkei 225 climbed 1.79%, while the broader Topix index gained 1.07%. South Korea's Kospi advanced 1.52%, although the small-cap Kosdaq slipped 0.42%.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO listing today: Here's what GMP indicates ahead of stock market debut
articles.summary: Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO is set to list today at 10:00 IST after receiving a muted response, subscribing 1.46 times overall. Retail participation was strong, with shares expected to list at a discount of 6.31% below the IPO price of ₹808.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: From Gift Nifty, US-Iran talks to fall in Japanese Yen: 8 key things that changed for Indian stock market overnight
articles.summary: Gift Nifty was trading around 23,994 level, a discount of nearly 16 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close, indicating a negative start for the Indian stock market indices.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: Check how Indian stock market is expected to trade on 1 July
articles.summary: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: The trends on Gift Nifty also indicate a tepid start for the Indian benchmark index. The Gift Nifty was trading around 23,994 level, a discount of nearly 16 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Advit Jewels IPO listing date today. Here’s what GMP signals on share debut
articles.summary: Advit Jewels IPO is set to list today, 1 July, at 10:00 IST, following a record subscription of 212.63 times. The share price is estimated at ₹187.5, indicating a strong debut. The funds will strengthen the company's balance sheet and support growth.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Breakout stocks to buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends five shares to buy today - 1 July 2026
articles.summary: The Indian stock market ended Tuesday's session on a lower note. Sumeet Bagadia of Choice Broking recommends five breakout stocks to buy today.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Raja Venkatraman recommends three stocks for 1 July
articles.summary: Market expert Raja Venkatraman shares his top stock picks for 1 July. Here’s his technical outlook and trade strategy.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stock recommendations for 1 July from MarketSmith India
articles.summary: MarketSmith India reveals its top stock recommendations for today, 1 July. Get expert insights into the best-performing stocks to guide your investment decisions.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Manufacturing companies eclipse consumer firms in a cooling IPO market
articles.summary: Companies raised a record ₹1.96 trillion through 91 mainboard initial public offerings (IPOs) in 2024, followed by another ₹1.76 trillion across a record 103 issues in 2025. But activity cooled in 2026 amid volatile markets and geopolitical uncertainty, with 23 IPOs raising ₹21,506 crore so far.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India Inc eyes more dollar debt as RBI policy support kicks in
articles.summary: Driven by central bank moves in February and June to lower hedging costs and ease rules, local firms are aggressively swapping out rupee loans for offshore funds.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Companies return to bond market as yields ease, RBI’s move opens up domestic debt markets
articles.summary: Easier external commercial borrowing norms and hedging costs for select public sector undertakings are expected to shift a portion of PSU fundraising offshore, reducing crowding in the domestic bond market.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nike shares slip ahead of Q4 results as investors await signs of turnaround
articles.summary: At 1:14 p.m. EDT, Nike stock was trading 0.22% lower, down $0.09 at $41.39. The shares have fallen 34.66% so far this year, reflecting investor concerns over slowing sales, weak demand in key markets and broader economic uncertainty
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: KPIT Technologies flags weak June-quarter performance; margins under pressure
articles.summary: KPIT Technologies projects a revenue drop in Q1FY27 due to European OEM adjustments. Still, the company is optimistic for FY27, emphasizing strong business fundamentals, ongoing growth in various technology domains, and strategies for margin improvement through AI innovations.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold slips below $4,000, silver falls $1.6 amid US Fed rate hike fears
articles.summary: Gold and silver prices fluctuated on June 30, driven down by a strong US dollar and interest rate hike expectations. Gold fell to $3,955, nearing a 12% monthly decline, while silver dropped 21% in June, reflecting overall market correction amid geopolitical tensions and changing investor sentiment.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: AI-fuelled chip stock rally hits speed bump despite record quarterly gains
articles.summary: Micron, Intel, AMD, and Sandisk have faced pressure amid concerns about the sustainability of AI-driven stock performance, leading to a global tech sell-off in June. Despite recent declines, semiconductor stocks are still set for record quarterly performances due to strong AI demand.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Wall Street edges higher on final day of stellar quarter, Nvidia rises 1%, Concentrix sheds 17.7%
articles.summary: As of 10:05 a.m. Eastern Time, the S&P 500 rose 0.3%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average was up 0.1%, and the Nasdaq Composite was 0.7% higher
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Small-cap multibagger Cupid Ltd extends rally; stock surges 47% in June, 800% in a year
articles.summary: Cupid's stock surged 800% in the past year, fueled by strong investor interest and robust market performance, becoming one of India's best-performing stocks. The company's revenue guidance for FY27 has been raised, reflecting confidence in its diversified business model and growth opportunities.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: US stocks poised for strongest quarterly performance in six years as AI optimism lifts markets
articles.summary: As of 7:12 a.m. New York time, S&P 500 futures were up 0.2%, while Nasdaq 100 futures gained 0.4%. Dow Jones Industrial Average futures also traded 0.2% higher
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty 50 sheds 9% in H1CY26: Is the Indian stock market ripe for a solid rebound in the second half of the year?
articles.summary: Nifty 50 has suffered a massive loss of almost 9% in the first half of the calendar year 2026. Despite a challenging first half of 2026, experts predict a potential recovery for the Indian stock market in the latter half of the year.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq edge higher as tech rally extends; Wall Street eyes strong H1 finish
articles.summary: US stock futures rose as technology and chip stocks continued to rebound, helping Wall Street secure a strong first half of the year. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 saw notable gains, driven by optimism in AI investments, despite geopolitical and inflation concerns.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty IT hits over 3-year low, crashes 30% year-to-date. Is it the right time to buy IT stocks?
articles.summary: With the Nifty IT index hitting a 3-year low, analysts share insights on the evolving landscape of the sector. Despite cautious client spending and economic uncertainties, opportunities in AI-led services may present a compelling case for long-term investors. Is it the right moment to buy?
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Radhakishan Damani portfolio: These two dividend stocks yield consistent cash for investors
articles.summary: Dividend-paying stocks attract long-term investors seeking income and capital appreciation. Radhakishan Damani's portfolio includes VST Industries and Advani Hotels, both with strong dividend histories.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rolex Rings sets July 3 as record date for ₹180-crore share buyback
articles.summary: Rolex Rings will conduct a share buyback of up to 10 million equity shares at ₹180 each, with July 3, 2026, as the record date. The buyback follows a board approval and aims to stabilize stock prices amid recent declines.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stock market today: Sensex, Nifty 50 extend losses for 2nd consecutive session; mid, small-caps outperform
articles.summary: Stock market today: The Sensex ended 250 points, or 0.33%, lower at 76,478.67, while the Nifty 50 dropped 81 points, or 0.34%, to settle at 23,865.75. The Nifty Midcap 100 index climbed 0.37%, while the Smallcap 100 index jumped 1.02%.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Penny stock under ₹10 hits 5% upper circuit. Details here
articles.summary: A-1 Limited's shares reached ₹7.30, capped at a 5% limit, after becoming the primary dealer for Ishan Dyes' sulphur chemicals. This partnership enhances A-1's market presence and is expected to drive revenue growth in FY2026-27 and beyond.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold vs Silver: Which commodity offers better return potential in H2 CY26?
articles.summary: During the first half of CY26, Comex gold prices have fallen more than 7%, while silver has corrected over 16%. In the domestic market, while MCX gold price has gained roughly 4% on a in 2026 so far, MCX silver price has declined more than 5% during the same period.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Economy holds firm, but risks remain from monsoon and geopolitics, says govt's economic review
articles.summary: The West Asia conflict and deficient monsoon rainfall so far underscore the need to reorient India's agricultural pricing policies to incentivise the cultivation of climate-resilient crops
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India's fiscal deficit at 9.6% of FY27 target in May
articles.summary: The government has budgeted a fiscal deficit of 4.3% of GDP, or about ₹16.96 trillion, for FY27 and is expected to rely on stronger tax collections and non-tax receipts in the coming months to keep the deficit within the target.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Morgan Stanley cuts oil price forecast again as Strait of Hormuz flows recover, warns of glut
articles.summary: Morgan Stanley has downgraded its oil forecasts as Strait of Hormuz traffic normalizes quicker than expected, coupled with strong US supplies and weak Chinese demand, risking an oil glut. Brent prices are expected to average $75 per barrel for the next two quarters.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The human cost of India’s new welfare architecture, explained in charts
articles.summary: India is shifting from rights-based welfare to new welfarism, prioritizing centralized cash transfers and tangible goods over public services. This transition strains state finances and risks eroding long-term human development.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The women behind India’s economy—and the price of invisible work
articles.summary: A recent Supreme Court judgment places economic value on the work done by homemakers. That value won’t make it into GDP math, but should find its way into policymaking.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Freight rates to stay elevated amid high demand for tankers, vessels
articles.summary: Global shipping freight rates are expected to stay high for the coming months due to increased demand from recovering trade flows and the peak shipping season, despite a slight easing in rates following the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Industrial output grows 5.1% in May under new IIP series with 2022-23 base
articles.summary: Growth was led by a robust expansion in manufacturing and supported by moderate growth in mining and electricity, according to provisional data released by the statistics ministry on Monday.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India regains top spot in EM tracker, but higher cost pressures continue to linger
articles.summary: Stronger exports and factory activity helped India reclaim the top spot in Mint's Emerging Markets Tracker. However, higher crude prices during the West Asia war have raised wholesale inflation and input costs, delaying relief for businesses despite the recent easing in oil prices.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: North-East floods raise power, logistics and inflation risks
articles.summary: The disruptions could have wider consequences for the rest of the country, where power demand remains high amid persistent heat and an uneven monsoon.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Modi spotlights shift to public transport, natural fertilizers amid Gulf crisis
articles.summary: Addressing the nation in his monthly radio broadcast ‘Mann Ki Baat’, Modi said that following his appeal to avoid foreign travel and cut gold purchases, many people have postponed overseas holidays and chosen not to buy new gold, with some instead recycling old gold to make new jewellery.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt eases Quality Control Order rules: Which industry segments are likely to get relief?
articles.summary: The government's new transition framework offers relief to manufacturers in sectors such as toys, personal protective equipment (PPE), air conditioners, footwear, furniture and select electrical appliances.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: AI job apocalypse ‘unlikely,' could create new opportunities over time: Goldman Sachs report
articles.summary: A Goldman Sachs report says fears of an AI-driven jobs apocalypse are exaggerated, despite significant labour market disruption ahead. Experts expect AI to displace some workers while creating new jobs over time.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Iranian oil may return, but Indian refiners eye concessions
articles.summary: As Indian refiners await clarity in terms of payment mechanism and long-term durability of the US-Iran peace talks, they may not rush for immediate procurement of Iranian crude and are taking a more long-term approach, to ensure supplies when the sanctions are permanently waived off
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Will water shortages derail India’s AI Data Centre boom?
articles.summary: India's ambition to become a leading AI data centre hub brings unprecedented investments, but it also raises crucial questions about sustainability.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The week in charts: Weak core output, Russian crude imports, AI edge
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: For better outcomes, Centre nudges states on focused capex for ₹2 tn loan scheme
articles.summary: The DoE has asked states and UTs to limit their proposals under its SASCI scheme for capex to their choice of maximum five priority sectors in FY27. The move marks a shift from simply encouraging higher capex to ensuring more concentrated investments that deliver measurable development outcomes.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Global giants backing India’s shipbuilding boom, says shipping secretary
articles.summary: Driven by a ₹70,000-crore policy package, domestic yards have secured huge export orders while the government backs five massive manufacturing clusters to scale up capacity, Vijay Kumar, secretary at the shipping ministry, told Mint in an exclusive interview.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Businesses embrace GST, but seek more reforms: Deloitte survey
articles.summary: The findings come at a time when GST collections continue to be robust. In May, the Centre and states together collected ₹1.94 trillion in gross GST revenue, before adjusting for refunds, up 3.2% from ₹1.88 trillion mopped up a year ago.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Why India’s consumers may stay cautious despite easing oil prices
articles.summary: Falling crude oil prices offer hope, but delayed rains, food inflation and higher living costs continue to weaken consumer confidence.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Millets, check dams and emergency plans: How India is preparing for dry days
articles.summary: Low monsoon rainfall is significant, as the season accounts for about 70% of India's annual rainfall. The rural economy, which relies heavily on agriculture, remains an important driver of economic growth.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: RBI allows banks to give loans for FCNR deposits
articles.summary: On 5 June, RBI said it will absorb the foreign exchange hedging cost on fresh  CNR (B), deposits, creating an unusual opportunity for overseas Indians
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Moving decisively towards finalizing trade deal with India, says US Ambassador Gor
articles.summary: India and the US are moving closer to finalizing a bilateral trade agreement, with commerce minister Piyush Goyal and USTR Jamieson Greer holding talks in New Delhi. Negotiations focus on tariffs, market access and digital trade ahead of a key US tariff deadline in July.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India's private sector output moderates further in June
articles.summary: June saw slower growth in new orders, international sales, employment and business activity.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Will Singapore remain India's export star even after Iran war ends?
articles.summary: West Asian geopolitical tensions have driven a surge in India’s exports to Singapore, utilizing its robust transshipment infrastructure to diversify from traditional hubs. Will this continue?
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt plans subsidized tomato sales to cool price surge
articles.summary: In several local retail markets, tomatoes are currently being sold at ₹60-70 per kg. Retail tomato prices have jumped in India due to delays in arrivals and weather-related disruptions in producing regions.
articles.label: NEUTRAL
